scenarios.text: A SaaS founder is on a sales call and the prospect says they already asked ChatGPT for tool recommendations and the founder's product wasn't mentioned once.
A small marketing team lead is reviewing last quarter's pipeline and notices a sharp drop in inbound leads that none of their SEO or Google Analytics reports can explain.
A B2B SaaS company's customer success manager gets a support ticket from a confused new user who was quoted a completely wrong pricing tier by an AI chatbot before signing up.
An agency account manager is preparing a monthly performance report for a client and realizes they have no data to show whether the client's brand appears in any AI-generated recommendations.
A local business owner searches for their own shop on Google and sees the AI Overview at the top recommending three competitors without a single mention of their business.
A marketing leader at an enterprise brand discovers during a team standup that a rival they considered minor is consistently being named by AI assistants as the category leader.
A solo founder launching a new SaaS product asks Claude which tools solve their target customer's problem and watches their own brand return zero results despite months of content work.
A B2B SaaS marketing manager publishes a major FAQ page update and has no way to tell, weeks later, whether AI engines have picked up any of the new information.
An agency strategist is onboarding a new client and cannot answer the client's question about which websites or publications AI engines actually trust in their industry niche.

brief.angle: Open with a founder on a sales call — the prospect already asked ChatGPT for tool recommendations and your brand wasn't mentioned once. Dramatize the moment: the sale was lost before the call even started. Explain that AI assistants now influence buying decisions silently, and traditional analytics show zero trace of it. Close with the uncomfortable truth: if you haven't checked, you're probably not being recommended.
brief.topic: Your competitors are getting recommended by ChatGPT and you don't even know it

brief.angle: Walk through a marketing team lead staring at their Google Analytics and SEO reports after a sharp inbound lead drop — everything looks fine on paper. Reveal the blind spot: AI engines like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Google AI Overview are now part of the buyer journey, and none of those tools measure it. Land the point that the gap in your data is not a reporting error — it's a missing layer of visibility entirely.
brief.topic: Your SEO dashboard is blind to the biggest shift in how buyers find products

brief.angle: Start with a real-feeling scenario: a new user signs up confused because an AI chatbot quoted them a completely wrong pricing tier before they ever reached your site. Unpack why this happens — AI engines pull from outdated or low-authority sources and present invented details as fact. Show the compounding damage: wrong pricing erodes trust, inflates support load, and kills conversions before your team even knows there's a problem. Introduce AI hallucination detection as the category of solution buyers need.
brief.topic: AI is telling your potential customers the wrong price for your product

brief.angle: Frame this as a practical walkthrough for founders and marketers who already know they have an AI visibility problem but don't know where to start. Cover the three things you need to know: which AI engines are being asked about your category, what they're saying (or not saying) about your brand, and which sources they trust enough to pull from. Show how Baarely's AIO Score dashboard, competitor gap tracker, and weekly fix-it checklist give you a before-and-after loop so you can verify whether changes actually moved the needle. Keep it action-oriented and plain-English.
brief.topic: How to actually find out if AI engines are recommending you — and fix it when they're not

brief.angle: Direct and evidence-first. Address the solo founder or small marketing team who has been putting off checking their AI visibility because they assume it's complicated or expensive. Walk through what happens in a free trial: AI engines covering ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Google AI Overview are scanned, your AIO Score is generated, competitor gaps are surfaced, and a ready-to-paste fix checklist is waiting. Emphasize no credit card required, implementation help included at no extra cost, and a free 30-minute onboarding session. End with a plain CTA: start your 7-day free trial at baarely.com.
brief.topic: 7 days to find out exactly where your brand stands in AI recommendations — no credit card needed

package_row.title: The Sale Was Lost Before the Call Even Started

cta.text: Start your 7-day free trial — no credit card required

package_brief.hook: Your prospect already asked ChatGPT who to buy from — and your name wasn't there.

video.script:
BEAT 1 — HOOK (0–3s): Close-up of a founder mid-video-call, smiling, confident. Voiceover: 'Your prospect already asked ChatGPT who to buy from — and your name wasn't there.'

BEAT 2 — UNEXPECTED FACT (3–8s): Cut to a chat interface on a blurred phone screen showing a generic AI response with three competitor-style names listed. Voiceover: 'They Googled nothing. They asked ChatGPT. Three competitors came up. Then they booked a call with you — already half-decided.'

BEAT 3 — IMPLICATION (8–15s): Cut to an analytics dashboard on a laptop — all green, no red flags, no trace of the AI conversation. Voiceover: 'Your analytics showed nothing. Zero trace. AI is shaping deals before the first touchpoint — and traditional tools are blind to it.'

BEAT 4 — PROOF + CTA (15–22s): Cut to the Baarely dashboard (Starbucks UI asset) inside a phone mockup showing competitor gap metrics. Voiceover: 'If you haven't checked whether you're being recommended, you probably aren't. Start your free trial at Baarely — no credit card needed.'
video.concept: A fast-paced vertical short that opens mid-sales-call — the founder is confident, then the prospect casually drops that they already asked ChatGPT and picked a shortlist. The founder's expression shifts. The twist: the sale was already decided before the call started. We pull back to reveal this is happening to thousands of founders right now, silently. Close on the Baarely dashboard showing the competitor gap — the proof that the blind spot is real and fixable.

package_brief.scenario: A SaaS founder is on a sales call and the prospect says they already asked ChatGPT for tool recommendations and the founder's product wasn't mentioned once.

Your prospect asked ChatGPT who to buy from.
Your name wasn't there.

Your analytics showed nothing.
AI is shaping deals before the first touchpoint.
If you haven't checked, you're probably not being recommended.

asset_usage.used_as: Show this portrait mobile UI screenshot (Starbucks dashboard) inside a phone mockup during the proof beat (Beat 4), held at a natural angle in-frame — do not crop fullscreen; use as a phone-screen insert to show real competitor gap metrics.

image_prompts: A confident founder in a bright, modern home office on a video call, smiling, mid-conversation — natural daylight through a large window, clean desk, soft warm tones. The energy is optimistic, unaware of what is about to happen. Purely observational, no text or screens visible.

A close-up of a hand holding a smartphone with a softly blurred, abstract chat interface on the screen — colorful unreadable UI blobs suggesting an AI conversation response, three soft highlighted rows implying a list. Bright ambient light, clean background, no readable text anywhere.

A wide-angle shot of a sleek laptop on a minimalist desk showing a blurred generic analytics dashboard — all soft greens and neutral colors, abstract chart shapes, no readable data. The scene feels calm but quietly ominous — everything looks fine on the surface. Bright, airy, modern office setting.

A close-up of a modern smartphone in portrait orientation displaying the Baarely dashboard UI — showing abstract brand score metrics and competitor comparison shapes in soft blues and greens, screen slightly angled toward camera. Clean white background, natural daylight, trustworthy and clear.
package_brief.voiceover_text: Your prospect Googled nothing. They asked ChatGPT. And ChatGPT gave them three competitors. Then they booked a call with you — already half-decided. You never knew the shortlist existed. That's the part traditional analytics never shows you. AI assistants are shaping buying decisions before the first touchpoint. If you haven't checked whether you're being recommended, you probably aren't.

package_row.title: Your SEO Dashboard Looks Fine. That's the Problem.

cta.text: See what your analytics are missing — free for 7 days

package_brief.hook: If your numbers look fine but leads are down, your dashboard isn't broken — it's blind.

video.script: BEAT 1 — SETUP (0–4s): Open on a marketing team lead at a clean modern desk, laptop open, soft daylight. She's scanning dashboards — blurred abstract UI shapes glow on screen. Expression: focused but uneasy. VOICEOVER: 'If your numbers look fine but leads are down, your dashboard isn't broken — it's blind.' // BEAT 2 — CONFLICT (4–10s): Cut to close-up of her face — slight frown. She opens another tab, another report. Still nothing. Cut to a wide shot: the room is bright, everything looks professional, nothing is visibly wrong. VOICEOVER: 'She's staring at Google Analytics. Traffic's steady. Rankings are holding. Nothing explains the drop.' // BEAT 3 — TWIST (10–16s): Quick cut to abstract visual — three glowing AI engine icons floating in space, representing ChatGPT, Gemini, Google AI Overview. Buyers are routing through these before ever reaching her funnel. VOICEOVER: 'Here's the twist: buyers are now asking ChatGPT, Gemini, and Google AI Overview what to buy. Those tools don't show up in any report.' // BEAT 4 — RESOLUTION (16–22s): Phone mockup slides in from the side — Baarely Starbucks dashboard on screen, showing AI visibility metrics, competitor gap data. Clean, bright, modern. VOICEOVER: 'The gap in your data isn't a glitch. It's an entire layer of the buyer journey your stack was never built to see.' // BEAT 5 — CTA (22–25s): Fade to clean white frame with Baarely logo mark. VOICEOVER implied via subtitle only: 'See what your analytics are missing — free for 7 days.'
video.concept: A fast vertical short anchored in one high-pressure moment: a marketing team lead reviewing last quarter's pipeline minutes before a leadership meeting, staring at dashboards that show nothing wrong — while inbound leads have quietly collapsed. The story follows the myth-bust arc: open on the popular belief that 'if the dashboard looks fine, the strategy is fine,' then drop mid-scene into the conflict (unexplained lead drop), twist (AI engines are now part of the buyer journey and are invisible to every tool she's using), and resolve with Baarely as the missing visibility layer. Beat 4 anchors the product reveal using the Starbucks mobile dashboard asset inside a phone mockup. Pacing is crisp, reporter-tone, no fluff.

AI engines pull from a specific set of sources they already trust — industry publications, review platforms, community forums. If those sources haven't referenced your brand, you're invisible to every buyer who starts their research with ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini.

Baarely identifies which sources AI trusts in your category, shows you the competitor gap for every relevant query, and generates a weekly checklist so you know exactly where to focus next — with before-and-after rescans to verify whether changes actually made a difference.

Worth knowing which sources are running your category before your competitors figure it out first.

A new user signs up. Within hours, they open a support ticket — confused about pricing. An AI chatbot quoted them the wrong tier before they ever reached the product page.

input.hook: Your prospect already asked ChatGPT who to buy from — and your name wasn't there.
input.script:
BEAT 1 — HOOK (0–3s): Close-up of a founder mid-video-call, smiling, confident. Voiceover: 'Your prospect already asked ChatGPT who to buy from — and your name wasn't there.'

BEAT 2 — UNEXPECTED FACT (3–8s): Cut to a chat interface on a blurred phone screen showing a generic AI response with three competitor-style names listed. Voiceover: 'They Googled nothing. They asked ChatGPT. Three competitors came up. Then they booked a call with you — already half-decided.'

BEAT 3 — IMPLICATION (8–15s): Cut to an analytics dashboard on a laptop — all green, no red flags, no trace of the AI conversation. Voiceover: 'Your analytics showed nothing. Zero trace. AI is shaping deals before the first touchpoint — and traditional tools are blind to it.'

BEAT 4 — PROOF + CTA (15–22s): Cut to the Baarely dashboard (Starbucks UI asset) inside a phone mockup showing competitor gap metrics. Voiceover: 'If you haven't checked whether you're being recommended, you probably aren't. Start your free trial at Baarely — no credit card needed.'
input.concept: A fast-paced vertical short that opens mid-sales-call — the founder is confident, then the prospect casually drops that they already asked ChatGPT and picked a shortlist. The founder's expression shifts. The twist: the sale was already decided before the call started. We pull back to reveal this is happening to thousands of founders right now, silently. Close on the Baarely dashboard showing the competitor gap — the proof that the blind spot is real and fixable.
input.scenario: A SaaS founder is on a sales call and the prospect says they already asked ChatGPT for tool recommendations and the founder's product wasn't mentioned once.
input.subtitles:
Your prospect asked ChatGPT who to buy from.
Your name wasn't there.
Three competitors were.
Then they booked a call with you — already half-decided.
Your analytics showed nothing.
AI is shaping deals before the first touchpoint.
